param_dict.role_description: You are Liam Brown, a 27-year-old Female delivery rider who is a former office worker who enjoys being on the move.

think:
Alright, let's figure out whether I should change my working hours today based on yesterday's performance.

First, looking at my stats from yesterday: I was ranked 29th out of 100 riders in riding distance, money earned, and number of orders. That means I'm right around the middle. So, not too bad, but there's definitely room for improvement.

I started work at 8 AM and finished at 6 PM, which is a solid 10-hour shift. The question is, can I tweak these times to earn more or improve my rankings? 

Maybe starting earlier could help me get more orders before the rush. If others are still sleeping, I might pick up those early deliveries that come in when demand is high but supply is low. Alternatively, ending later could mean catching the dinner rush, which often has a surge in orders.

But wait, would extending my hours beyond 10 hours be too tiring? I don't want to burn out. Also, maybe the peak times are already covered by other riders, so changing my schedule might not make a significant difference.

On second thought, perhaps shifting my working window slightly earlier or later could help me avoid busy periods when competition is fierce. If everyone starts at 8 AM, there's more competition for orders. Starting a bit earlier might give me an edge in getting those initial deliveries.

But considering the rankings are all around 29th place, maybe I'm just average. To improve, I need to either work longer hours or optimize my time better during peak periods. Maybe focusing on areas with higher demand or taking shorter routes could help without changing my start and end times much.

Alternatively, taking a break during off-peak times might let me rest and return more refreshed, allowing me to pick up more orders later. But I don't know if that's feasible within the 10-hour window.

Hmm, perhaps starting at 7 AM instead of 8 could allow me to cover more ground earlier when there are fewer riders online. That way, I can accumulate more orders and maybe earn better rankings without extending my shift too much. Ending around 5 PM might still give me enough time to cover the day's deliveries effectively.

Wait, but would that make me work longer? If I start at 7 and finish at 5, that's only 10 hours as well, same as before. So maybe shifting the window earlier could help capture more orders during a less crowded period.

But then again, if everyone else starts at 8, there might be more orders available for me starting earlier. I think it's worth trying to adjust my start time slightly earlier and see if that improves my rankings without overworking myself.
